h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Tail's drawn here though — not down at the wheels.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would simply accept the washers/chips distinction and complete the labeling.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Track on cart — up arrow. [taps blue chip beside arrow tail] Foot here. [taps washer at wheels] Touch there. Different spots. [glances at down arrow] Earth on cart — foot on the chip too. But Earth doesn't touch... no washer for that one.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner places labels correctly using the new representation and independently extends it by noting the missing washer for the non-contact force.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The touch card settled who pushes. It did not settle why the foot sits here. [places a washer at the wheels and a blue chip on the cart body, beside the arrow tail] Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force. Track card to the washer. Up-arrow name to the blue chip: force on cart. Then say the up arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: Tutor acknowledges the old route's insufficiency and introduces a new device with washers and chips to address the spatial confusion.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precisely fitted to the learner's confusion, providing a clear, actionable distinction with concrete physical markers.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No later tutor turn after the learner pivot.
